headline: Clemson and BC Both Seek Redemption After Week One Stumbles
subhead: Two ranked teams facing elimination pressure in a must-win ACC matchup.

xFactor.why: BC's ability to generate interior pressure will determine whether Clemson's rebuilt offensive line can establish rhythm downfield.

keyMatchup: Clemson's run game versus Boston College's run defense will dictate field position and third-down survival rates for both offenses.
body: Both Clemson and Boston College absorbed double-digit home losses in Week One, making this a genuine reset moment rather than a typical non-conference tuneup. Clemson's 35-20 defeat to Tennessee exposed unfinished business along the line of scrimmage and a secondary that looked backpedaling all evening. Boston College's 29-20 loss to Arkansas followed a similar script: defensible margin, preventable mistakes, and the sense that execution wasn't yet syndicated.

The Tigers carry the weight of a top-ten ranking into Chestnut Hill and should have the personnel advantage, but BC's front seven can make Clemson uncomfortable if they stay gap-sound. Whoever wins this game avoids falling to 0-2 and maintains theoretical ACC contention through September. For a Clemson team with championship expectations, a road win steadies the narrative. For BC, a landmark victory over a ranked ACC rival would suggest last week was merely a sloppy opener rather than a sign of deeper structural problems.
